dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal emphasizes equal priority for both long and short entries in trending conditions with no directional bias. Used ADX_14 > 25 to confirm trending regime applies to both directions. Exit uses trend strength deterioration (ADX < 20). The core logic is regime-based activation of bidirectional signal generation rather than specific technical entry conditions; this translation captures the 'always active in trends' concept through trend confirmation. No quality score ranking mechanism available in standard indicator set, so allocation prioritizes whichever direction is in confirmed trend.

Used ADX_14 > 25 to confirm trending regime applies to both directions. Exit uses trend strength deterioration (ADX < 20). The core logic is regime-based activation of bidirectional signal generation rather than specific technical entry conditions; this translation captures the 'always active in trends' concept through trend confirmation. No quality score ranking mechanism available in standard indicator set, so allocation prioritizes whichever direction is in confirmed trend.

source_rule_text: A trader should expand market exposure to include both long and short opportunities rather than restricting themselves to one direction, because trending opportunities exist in both directions and constraining oneself eliminates half of available profitable setups.
